title: handoff-receipt@1
description: Envelope-trust handoff receipt. A producer self-issued declaration of identity, well-formedness, provenance, and a validity window. The schema deliberately has no field capable of carrying a verdict, an approval, a confidence score, or an independence claim: the only place those words may appear is never_attests.

synthetic.description: Optional example-marking flag for committed synthetic fixtures. Carries no semantics; consumers MUST NOT branch on it.

sha256.description: Content pin: sha256 of the producer SKILL.md at emit time.

id.description: <subject-class-or-hash>-<YYYYMMDD>-<seq>. The slug segment is the same subject-class-or-hash as subject.ref, never free text.

at.description: Day-granularity date (YYYY-MM-DD). No predicate consumes finer precision.

session.description: Equality token: keyed hash recomputed per run under a session-scoped in-memory key. The key and the raw harness session id are never persisted.

matched.description: Closed trigger-class vocabulary; never verbatim user prose.

revision.description: git-SHA, doc-version, or null. Null subjects fail closed: a null revision MUST NOT be combined with the subject-revision-unchanged predicate (verifier-enforced).

sha256.description: sha256 of the frozen subject artifact, or null.

valid_while.description: Closed predicate vocabulary, evaluated by the consumer. Predicates are added only by schema-version bump + verifier update in the same PR; unknown predicates fail closed.

coverage_limits.description: What this run did NOT check, verbatim from the skill's own degradation labels.

never_attests.description: Non-empty. Standard entries: verdict-truth, independence-achieved, freshness-beyond-window.
